title: Chapter Forty-Eight: The Lazy Flame Dragon
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Yeon-shin stared at him.

The young master's energy was a different species from his siblings'. Where the second son had shown refined blood energy and disciplined sword paths, this man radiated heat like a forge at full blast -- as if his blood vessels were filled with molten iron instead of qi.

He dropped Hwangbo Myeong-rin.

Not gently. He shook his hand and she sprawled backward, cosmetic powder on his palm from where he had struck her face. Once, twice, three times -- quick blows with Jeong Family Dynamic Technique threading minute true energy through each impact. Enough to shake her skull. Not enough to kill.

She was his secondary arrangement. If the young master proved difficult, Hwangbo Myeong-rin's fury would ensure the family mobilized.

"Oh my," the young master said. His smile did not waver. "Already troublesome. Thinking of the family elders."

His hand extended. It looked casual -- the reaching arm of a man trying to separate brawlers.

It was not casual.

Like drunken fist. Feints indistinguishable from real strikes. The hand aimed for Yeon-shin's arm where it gripped Myeong-rin's collar.

*This is what I came for.*

Yeon-shin layered Fate Defying Scripture true energy through his right arm's fine meridians. He shook Myeong-rin free and met the young master's strike.

*Puk puk!*

Hand shadows tangled. The power in the young master's arms was immense -- heavy, hot, each contact searing through protective energy.

*How can he contain such scorching yang energy in his blood vessels? It should be tearing him apart.*

*Supreme rising martial arts.*

Yeon-shin raised another stream of true energy, surrounding his arm with an invisible barrier. The heat dulled slightly.

*Pak!*

The exchange accelerated. Both trying to seize the other's wrist. The young master's golden silk hand moved with devastating complexity -- feints layered on feints, transitions that flowed like water over stones.

*It feels like exchanging moves with myself. A version of myself with more years and more pain.*

"My goodness," the young master said, his smile deepening. A flicker of something alive beneath those shadowed eyes. "There was another one like me?"

*He has leisure to talk. His power and experience are greater.*

Yeon-shin was being pushed. The internal energy in the young master's strikes grew endlessly heavier. His palm met each blow and was driven back, incrementally, inexorably.

*Puk!*

Powerful rotating force waves burst between them. Yeon-shin's hair scattered backward. He had answered with Eternal Blossom Fist's rotational strike -- palm to palm -- but the returning rebound drove him back a full step.

One step.

The young master had not moved.

"Attending the Dragon Phoenix Gathering?" He stood diagonally, one leg planted, flask still in hand. "Gu Gwa of the Chasing Truth Sect. I will remember. You would be quite worth watching as the years accumulate."

"And you?" Yeon-shin said, his breathing even despite the exertion. "Will you be watching?"

"I will not be able to see." The languid smile turned strange. "A grave to cool my body awaits. Already built big. I just need to go lie down in it."

*He knows he is dying.*

The words were free of self-pity. Free of bravado. Stated as plain fact, the way a man might describe the weather.

"Forgive my younger brother and sister moderately," the young master said. "In about ten years they will not be able to face you. They will have to look up quite a bit. Since they earn their own beatings."

He turned and trudged away, chewing the opium poppy, flask swinging from loose fingers. He did not look back at his beaten siblings.

*No attachment. No rage. A family heir who does not care about the family.*

"Seems like he should be in Desolate Sect," Yeon-shin murmured. "Why did he not seek the World Tree's fruit?"

Hyeon Won-chang's hand settled on his shoulder. "Are you alright? I was about to step in, but you looked strangely happy. You are also most skilled at provocation."

"You saw right. I do not understand the latter part."

Shin So-bin cut in, her tied hair flipped to reveal a sweating nape. "The Hwangbo young master's title is Lazy Flame Dragon. Counted among the strongest talents north of the Yangtze. He conquered the Dragon Phoenix Gathering at twenty -- five more years have passed. Even White Qilin Namgung Hwa-sin refuses to fight him."

"He lived five more years past twenty?"

"There are nine years between you. You have four years left until the coming-of-age ceremony."

"Four years."

*Four years.*

The door rattled. He kept it shut.

His stomach had gone sour, but his mind was already working. The Lazy Flame Dragon's technique -- the way he used wit instead of rigidity, the way his hands flowed between feints and true strikes without any visible transition.

*Senses can be used like that. No need to be trapped in forms.*

He had grown stronger from the exchange. One step, and he was better than he had been a heartbeat before.

"Let's go," Yeon-shin said.

He mounted his horse. Huizhou was close.

Behind him, the Hwangbo family party disappeared down the road. Hwangbo Myeong-rin's face -- frost-like resentment gathered there like ice forming on still water -- burned in his memory.

*She is my second arrangement. If provocation fails with the young master, Myeong-rin's fury will complete the mission.*

Shin So-bin pulled close, sound transmission vibrating from her lips.

*-Senior. Isn't the provocation a bit excessive? We should check nearby geography. If the Hwangbo family rushes in, it will become a chase.*

*Provocation? Which part?*

He had not even started.

---
